I agree with Option B because Type IV isthmi are defined by an isthmus linking three or more canals—either fully or partially—which aligns precisely with Kim's description. The presence of such structures poses significant challenges for successful root canal obturation due to their potential to harbor microorganisms even after instrumentation. Answer: B
